payload.query: It is well known in comparative studies of electoral behavior that we know much more about what explains elections than what elections explain for democratic politics. In an essay that addresses traditions of comparative voting behavior, address this issue. Select from such traditions as Values and Value Change, Social Cleavages (Lipset and Rokkan), the Standard American Voter Model, Economic Voting, or other relevant traditions.

articles.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War
articles.abstract: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.
articles.snippet: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.
articles.byline: By Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Edward Wong, Eric Schmitt and Ronen Bergman
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Iranians Viewed by U.S. as Potential Leaders Died in Strikes, Trump Says
articles.abstract: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.snippet: With the future of Iran deeply uncertain, President Trump acknowledged that it could end up with a ruler “as bad” as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
articles.byline: By Jim Tankersley, Aaron Boxerman, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
keywords: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

articles.title: Inside the Underground Safe Houses Sheltering Immigrants From ICE
articles.abstract: In Springfield, Ohio, some Americans have converted their basements and spare bedrooms into shelters for immigrant families who could be targeted in raids.
articles.snippet: In Springfield, Ohio, some Americans have converted their basements and spare bedrooms into shelters for immigrant families who could be targeted in raids.
articles.byline: By Miriam Jordan and Maddie McGarvey

articles.title: Trump, the Self-Declared Peace President, Goes to War Seeking Regime Change
articles.abstract: President Trump has become increasingly willing to assert American power overseas, a decade after propelling himself to the highest office by promising to focus on “America first.”
articles.snippet: President Trump has become increasingly willing to assert American power overseas, a decade after propelling himself to the highest office by promising to focus on “America first.”
keywords: United States Defense and Military Forces

articles.title: Trump Demands Iran’s ‘Unconditional Surrender’ as Israel Pounds Tehran and Lebanon
articles.abstract: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.
articles.snippet: Israel’s air forces shifted their focus back to Iran on Friday after a night of heavy bombardment in Lebanon, which was quickly becoming one of the largest fronts in the regional conflict.

articles.title: Behind Oklahoma Cannabis Farms, New Yorkers With Ties to Beijing
articles.abstract: In China, trafficking marijuana is punishable by death. But in the United States, it has become a lucrative sideline for some favored sons of the motherland.
articles.snippet: In China, trafficking marijuana is punishable by death. But in the United States, it has become a lucrative sideline for some favored sons of the motherland.
articles.byline: By Jay Root, Bianca Pallaro, Michael Forsythe and Shuran Huang

articles.title: Why Trump Always Thanks You ‘For Your Attention to This Matter’
articles.abstract: How a simple catchphrase sums up the president’s theory of executive power.
articles.snippet: How a simple catchphrase sums up the president’s theory of executive power.

articles.title: Trump Links His Push for Greenland to Not Winning Nobel Peace Prize
articles.abstract: In a text, President Trump told Norway’s prime minister that he no longer felt obliged to “think purely of Peace” and that the U.S. needed the island for global security.
articles.snippet: In a text, President Trump told Norway’s prime minister that he no longer felt obliged to “think purely of Peace” and that the U.S. needed the island for global security.
articles.byline: By Jeffrey Gettleman and Henrik Pryser Libell

articles.title: Starting With Formaldehyde, Trump Administration Reassesses Chemical Risks
articles.abstract: A draft memo from the E.P.A. assumes a safe threshold exists for formaldehyde, upending earlier findings that there is no safe level of exposure to the carcinogen.
articles.snippet: A draft memo from the E.P.A. assumes a safe threshold exists for formaldehyde, upending earlier findings that there is no safe level of exposure to the carcinogen.

articles.title: Focused More on Power Outages, Ukrainians See U.S. Peace Push as Noise
articles.abstract: Many understand the dance their leaders must perform to appease President Trump. But that doesn’t make them any less weary of the rounds and rounds of talks.
articles.snippet: Many understand the dance their leaders must perform to appease President Trump. But that doesn’t make them any less weary of the rounds and rounds of talks.
articles.byline: By Kim Barker and Laetitia Vançon
